title: Scale-Harmonic Reinterpretation of the Planck Scale — Empirical Validation
honestyBoundary: Architectural and numerical validation only. Prefix coincidence with SI Planck mantissa is not a derivation of quantum gravity. Full physics ontology claims remain unproven.

results.methodology: Standalone empirical suite for the Planck–1.6 EGS bridge: digit coincidence, sham mantissa nulls, Φ-scale recursion, singularity clamp, wave-operator stability, clutch reconstruction, and actual-vs-modelled multi-scale spectra.

experiments.title: Anchor prefix 1.6 and clutch gap Δ
experiments.interpretation: Prefix 1.6 (and 1.61) shared; at 3 decimals Φ truncates to 1.618 while Planck mantissa truncates to 1.616. Δ ≈ 0.00178 is the clutch / slip band.

experiments.title: Sham null — random mantissa coincidence with Φ
experiments.interpretation: Sharing truncated prefix 1.6 is common (~1.1% of random mantissas). Exact 3-decimal match to 1.618 is rarer (~0.011%). The Planck mantissa is close but not identical — clutch gap, not identity. SI unit choice also affects the mantissa.
experiments.honesty: Does NOT claim Φ is derived from Planck physics. Quantifies coincidence rate under a uniform mantissa null.

experiments.title: Φ-scale recursion ladder from l_P
experiments.interpretation: Φ-ladder yields constant log10 spacing ≈ 0.208; reaches meter scale near k ≈ 168. Continuous recursion without ad-hoc cutoffs in the tested window.

experiments.title: Singularity-free clamp at l_P (k=0)
experiments.interpretation: Clamping r ≥ l_P bounds every 1/r² term by 1/l_P² and keeps the radial potential sum finite. Unclamped approach to r=0 diverges. This supports the SynthOBS engine lower-bound claim as a numerical regularity prior — not a claim about quantum-gravity ontology.

experiments.title: Scale-harmonic wave operator discrete stability
experiments.interpretation: Φ-modulated CFL-safe schedule remains bounded; random speed sham tends to inflate energy / blow up. Supports the architectural wave-operator claim as a numerical regularity prior.

experiments.title: Clutch-band slip as resonant impedance play
experiments.interpretation: Within clutch Δ, load/source Φ-match keeps transfer efficiency η near 1. Outside the band (wild slip) efficiency drops and mean cascade loss rises — digits 3+ as bounded play, not free drift.

experiments.title: Actual-vs-modelled multi-scale spectrum (Φ ladder)
experiments.interpretation: On a Φ-generated multi-scale spectrum (with noise), the Φ model beats decade, √2, and random-base shams — actual-vs-modelled under a controlled generative ground truth.
